title: Functions, Callbacks, Context, and Memory Interfaces · Memory-Safety Defense
summary: A safety board presents unfamiliar functions, callbacks, context, and memory interfaces decisions across code, diagrams, diagnostics, and stakeholder evidence; each distractor corresponds to a named misconception. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for typed callback and context registry. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Declare and call function pointers with exactly compatible prototypes and return conventions.
Bind callback, context pointer, ownership, thread, and unregister lifetime into one interface contract.
Inject matched allocate, resize, and release operations with context and failure semantics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Function Pointer Compatibility
steps.instruction:
Order the Function Pointer Compatibility evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Function Pointer Compatibility.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Function Pointer Compatibility assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Function Pointer Compatibility, keep Function Pointer Compatibility connected to executable C memory invariant and explicit native transfer evidence.

content.text: A quiz native-systems team handles memory case 8790. Produce bounded C evidence to bind callback results to explicit context lifetime; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Declare and call function pointers with exactly compatible prototypes and return conventions.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Function Pointer Compatibility has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Declare and call function pointers with exactly compatible prototypes and return conventions.
